title: Vector Operations
code:
import numpy as np

# Define vectors
a = np.array([1, 2, 3])
b = np.array([4, 5, 6])

# Addition
print("a + b =", a + b)

# Scalar multiplication
print("2 * a =", 2 * a)

# Dot product
print("a · b =", np.dot(a, b))

# Cross product
print("a × b =", np.cross(a, b))

# Magnitude
print("|a| =", np.linalg.norm(a))
explanation: Demonstrates basic vector operations using NumPy library commonly used in linear algebra computations

title: Gaussian Elimination Algorithm
description: Algorithm to solve systems of linear equations
code:
ALGORITHM GaussianElimination(AUGMENTED_MATRIX [A|b], n)
BEGIN
 FOR i FROM 1 TO n DO
 # Find pivot
 max_row = i
 FOR j FROM i+1 TO n DO
 IF |A[j][i]| > |A[max_row][i]| THEN
 max_row = j
 END IF
 END FOR
 SWAP rows i and max_row
 
 # Make pivot 1 (optional for RREF)
 pivot = A[i][i]
 FOR j FROM i TO n+1 DO
 A[i][j] = A[i][j] / pivot
 END FOR
 
 # Eliminate below
 FOR j FROM i+1 TO n DO
 factor = A[j][i] / A[i][i]
 FOR k FROM i TO n+1 DO
 A[j][k] = A[j][k] - factor * A[i][k]
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 # Back substitution
 x[n] = A[n][n+1] / A[n][n]
 FOR i FROM n-1 DOWNTO 1 DO
 x[i] = A[i][n+1]
 FOR j FROM i+1 TO n DO
 x[i] = x[i] - A[i][j] * x[j]
 END FOR
 x[i] = x[i] / A[i][i]
 END FOR
 RETURN x
END
explanation: Forward elimination converts to upper triangular form, then back substitution finds solution. Time complexity O(n³) for elimination phase

title: Check Linear Independence
description: Algorithm to determine if set of vectors is linearly independent
code:
ALGORITHM CheckLinearIndependence(vectors[], n, m)
BEGIN
 # n = number of vectors, m = dimension
 # Form matrix with vectors as rows or columns
 MATRIX A[n][m]
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 FOR j FROM 0 TO m-1 DO
 A[i][j] = vectors[i][j]
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 # Compute rank using Gaussian elimination
 rank = ComputeRank(A)
 
 IF rank == n THEN
 RETURN "Linearly Independent"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Linearly Dependent"
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM ComputeRank(MATRIX A)
BEGIN
 row = 0
 FOR col FROM 0 TO m-1 DO
 # Find pivot row
 pivot = FindNonZeroRow(A, row, col)
 IF pivot != -1 THEN
 SWAP A[row] with A[pivot]
 Normalize row
 Eliminate other rows
 row = row + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN row
END
explanation: Vectors are linearly independent if their rank equals the number of vectors. Rank is computed by counting non-zero rows in row echelon form
